meta.originalTitle: The Clipper Races 帆船竞速

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage below.
The Clipper Races: an era of competition between cargo ships

During the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries, the British East India Company had the monopoly on trade with China and India. This meant that because no rival could legally import tea or other goods from these countries at this time, the company was rarely in a hurry to transport its merchandise. Instead, its priority was to minimise costs by carrying as much as possible on each ship. This meant that its ships – known as East Indiamen – were enormous, strong and very slow.

By 1800, the average East Indiaman could carry 1,200 tons of merchandise. The trading pattern for China tea usually meant the East Indiamen set sail from Britain in January, sailed round the Cape of Good Hope at the southernmost tip of Africa, and arrived in China in September. There they would load up that year's tea harvest, set off again and, depending on the wind and weather, aim to arrive back by the following September, so even with favourable sailing conditions, the round trip lasted almost two years, and if anything went wrong it could take a lot longer.

However, by 1834 the company had lost its trading monopolies, and tea had become a freely traded item. Having no more use for its great ships, the company sold them off, and many were bought by merchants or their captains, who continued to plough the seas between Britain and China. But now that tea could be traded freely, a few smart sailors began to realise that whoever brought each new harvest of tea to Britain first stood to make the most money.

This was partly because if you were home first, you could sell your shipment of tea before your competitors even arrived, and partly because consumers in Britain in the nineteenth century believed that the fresher and earlier-picked the tea, the better the resulting drink. Tea traders now needed faster, sleeker ships to bring their precious cargo back. Nevertheless, in Britain this idea only caught on slowly, and while the 1840s saw a few faster ships launched, for the time being many merchants remained satisfied with the slow but reliable East Indiamen.

In fact it was the Americans who pioneered the first clipper ships. These vessels were fast and slender, with a narrow hull that was deeper at the back than at the front, and masses of sails on tall masts. They earned their name from the way that they ‘clipped off' journey time. British merchants resolved to build their own clippers to rival the Americans, and the first British tea clipper, Stornaway, was built in Aberdeen in 1850. More tea clippers were designed and built in Britain throughout the 1850s and 1860s; they had a narrower beam than their American equivalents, making them less powerful during storms but faster in calmer weather.

There was a great spirit of competition between the British and American ships plying the tea trade, but to begin with the Americans had the edge. Then in 1851 a British shipowner, Richard Green, built the aptly named clipper Challenger with the stated intention of beating the American ships. Loaded with tea, Challenger left China for London in 1852 at the same time as the American clipper Challenge, a much larger, older ship already greatly admired for its speed. Large sums were bet on which would complete the journey first. In the event, the British ship beat its rival to London by two days, amid much jubilation. From then on, such international races grew in popularity.

After 1855, American participation in the British tea trade gradually stopped. But even without the Anglo-American rivalry, the competitive spirit continued. It was really ignited when new ports were opened up for trade in China. These included Foochow, which was much closer to the tea-producing areas than Canton, the port used previously. As a result, tea could be loaded on board earlier and fresher, and the clippers could set off in late May or early June – sometimes not even taking time to fill out the official paperwork – racing back to Britain whatever the difficulties.

They sped down through the South China Sea and into the Indian Ocean, then raced to get round the southernmost tip of Africa at the Cape of Good Hope. Then it was north across the vast Atlantic, past the Azores, through the English Channel and into the estuary of the River Thames. Once there, they would be towed by tugs up the river and into the docks.

The cargo of the winning ship could earn a premium of up to sixpence per pound – and so the captain and crew were rewarded by the owners of the cargo. But the races were about more than just money: the crews, about 40 men on each clipper, were expert sailors, proud of their ships, and they delighted in competing against each other. Without their enthusiasm, the races would never have happened, since getting home as fast as possible required the crew to be totally dedicated and to sacrifice much of their rest for the duration of the race.
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1–6 on your answer sheet, write
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information on this
In the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries, the British East India Company faced a lot of competition.
Before 1800, cargo size was the most important consideration for the East India Company.
At best, voyages of the East Indiamen to China and back took nearly two years to complete.
Before 1834, voyages to and from China were considered to be highly dangerous.
After 1834, the ships which had served the East India Company stopped being used for commercial purposes.
In the nineteenth century, British drinkers preferred tea made from mature leaves to that made from younger leaves.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：17-18 世纪，英国东印度公司面临激烈竞争。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “the British East India Company had the monopoly on trade with China and India”，“monopoly” 意为 “垄断”，说明公司无竞争，与题干 “面临激烈竞争” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：1800 年前，载货量是东印度公司最核心的考量因素。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “its priority was to minimise costs by carrying as much as possible on each ship”，“priority” 对应 “most important consideration”，“carrying as much as possible” 对应 “cargo size”，明确提到公司最关注载货量，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：东印度大帆船往返中国的航程，最快也需近 2 年完成。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “even with favourable sailing conditions, the round trip lasted almost two years”，“favourable sailing conditions” 对应 “at best”，“almost two years” 对应 “nearly two years”，明确提到最快航程近 2 年，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：1834 年前，往返中国的航程被认为极具危险性。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：文章 Paragraph 1-3 仅描述 1834 年前航程 “耗时久”，未提及 “航程危险”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：1834 年后，曾服务于东印度公司的船只不再用于商业用途。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Having no more use for its great ships, the company sold them off, and many were bought by merchants or their captains, who continued to plough the seas between Britain and China”，明确提到船只被商人或船长买下，继续用于英中航线（商业用途），与题干 “不再用于商业用途” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：19 世纪，英国饮茶者偏爱成熟茶叶制成的茶，而非嫩叶茶。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “consumers in Britain in the nineteenth century believed that the fresher and earlier-picked the tea, the better the resulting drink”，“earlier-picked” 对应 “younger leaves”，明确提到消费者偏爱 “早采摘的嫩叶茶”，与题干 “偏爱成熟茶叶” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：In the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries, the British East India Company faced a lot of competition.
题目翻译：17-18 世纪，英国东印度公司面临激烈竞争。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “the British East India Company had the monopoly on trade with China and India”，“monopoly” 意为 “垄断”，说明公司无竞争，与题干 “面临激烈竞争” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（2）题目 2：Before 1800, cargo size was the most important consideration for the East India Company.
题目翻译：1800 年前，载货量是东印度公司最核心的考量因素。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “its priority was to minimise costs by carrying as much as possible on each ship”，“priority” 对应 “most important consideration”，“carrying as much as possible” 对应 “cargo size”，明确提到公司最关注载货量，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（3）题目 3：At best, voyages of the East Indiamen to China and back took nearly two years to complete.
题目翻译：东印度大帆船往返中国的航程，最快也需近 2 年完成。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “even with favourable sailing conditions, the round trip lasted almost two years”，“favourable sailing conditions” 对应 “at best”，“almost two years” 对应 “nearly two years”，明确提到最快航程近 2 年，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（4）题目 4：Before 1834, voyages to and from China were considered to be highly dangerous.
题目翻译：1834 年前，往返中国的航程被认为极具危险性。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：文章 Paragraph 1-3 仅描述 1834 年前航程 “耗时久”，未提及 “航程危险”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（5）题目 5：After 1834, the ships which had served the East India Company stopped being used for commercial purposes.
题目翻译：1834 年后，曾服务于东印度公司的船只不再用于商业用途。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Having no more use for its great ships, the company sold them off, and many were bought by merchants or their captains, who continued to plough the seas between Britain and China”，明确提到船只被商人或船长买下，继续用于英中航线（商业用途），与题干 “不再用于商业用途” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（6）题目 6：In the nineteenth century, British drinkers preferred tea made from mature leaves to that made from younger leaves.
题目翻译：19 世纪，英国饮茶者偏爱成熟茶叶制成的茶，而非嫩叶茶。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “consumers in Britain in the nineteenth century believed that the fresher and earlier-picked the tea, the better the resulting drink”，“earlier-picked” 对应 “younger leaves”，明确提到消费者偏爱 “早采摘的嫩叶茶”，与题干 “偏爱成熟茶叶” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

children.text: from the passage for each answer.
Clipper ships were first used for trading by American merchants.
The ships were remarkable for the number of
The performance of British tea clippers was particularly affected when there were
It was in a ship called
that the British first competed successfully against the Americans.
Richard Green's ship arrived two days ahead of its competitor.
Competition increased when additional Chinese trading
Merchants were occasionally in such a hurry that they failed to complete the
At the end of their journey, the ships needed the help of
The crews were motivated by both
and their enthusiasm for the competition.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：这类船因其拥有的_\___\__数量而引人注目。
答案：sails（风帆）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “These vessels were fast and slender, with a narrow hull that was deeper at the back than at the front, and masses of sails on tall masts”，“masses of sails” 对应 “the number of sails”，明确提到船的显著特征是 “风帆数量多”，因此答案为 “sails”。
题目翻译：当海上出现_\___\__时，英国茶叶快速帆船的性能会受到显著影响。
答案：storms（风暴）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “they had a narrower beam than their American equivalents, making them less powerful during storms but faster in calmer weather”，“less powerful during storms” 对应 “performance... particularly affected”，明确提到风暴会影响英国快速帆船性能，因此答案为 “storms”。
题目翻译：英国人首次成功与美国人竞争，是在一艘名为_\___\__的船上。
答案：Challenger（挑战者号）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “in 1851 a British shipowner, Richard Green, built the aptly named clipper Challenger with the stated intention of beating the American ships... the British ship beat its rival to London by two days”，明确提到 “挑战者号（Challenger）” 是英国首艘击败美国船只的快速帆船，因此答案为 “Challenger”。
题目翻译：当中国新增贸易_\___\__建立后，竞争加剧。
答案：ports（港口）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “it was really ignited when new ports were opened up for trade in China”，“new ports” 对应 “additional... ports”，明确提到中国新增贸易港口引发竞争加剧，因此答案为 “ports”。
题目翻译：商人有时过于匆忙，离开中国前未能完成_\___\__。
答案：paperwork（文件手续）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “sometimes not even taking time to fill out the official paperwork”，“fill out the official paperwork” 对应 “complete the paperwork”，明确提到商人来不及完成官方文件手续，因此答案为 “paperwork”。
题目翻译：航程结束时，船只需要_\___\__的帮助。
答案：tugs（拖船）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “once there, they would be towed by tugs up the river and into the docks”，“towed by tugs” 对应 “needed the help of tugs”，明确提到船只需拖船协助进入码头，因此答案为 “tugs”。
题目翻译：船员的动力既来自_\___\__，也来自对竞争的热情。
答案：money（金钱）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “the cargo of the winning ship could earn a premium... and so the captain and crew were rewarded... But the races were about more than just money”，明确提到船员会获得金钱奖励，且竞争不止关乎金钱，即动力来自 “金钱” 与热情，因此答案为 “money”。

items.text:
（1）题目 7：The ships were remarkable for the number of \___\___ they had.
题目翻译：这类船因其拥有的_\___\__数量而引人注目。
答案：sails（风帆）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “These vessels were fast and slender, with a narrow hull that was deeper at the back than at the front, and masses of sails on tall masts”，“masses of sails” 对应 “the number of sails”，明确提到船的显著特征是 “风帆数量多”，因此答案为 “sails”。

（2）题目 8：The performance of British tea clippers was particularly affected when there were \___\___ at sea.
题目翻译：当海上出现_\___\__时，英国茶叶快速帆船的性能会受到显著影响。
答案：storms（风暴）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “they had a narrower beam than their American equivalents, making them less powerful during storms but faster in calmer weather”，“less powerful during storms” 对应 “performance... particularly affected”，明确提到风暴会影响英国快速帆船性能，因此答案为 “storms”。

（3）题目 9：It was in a ship called \___\___ that the British first competed successfully against the Americans.
题目翻译：英国人首次成功与美国人竞争，是在一艘名为_\___\__的船上。
答案：Challenger（挑战者号）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “in 1851 a British shipowner, Richard Green, built the aptly named clipper Challenger with the stated intention of beating the American ships... the British ship beat its rival to London by two days”，明确提到 “挑战者号（Challenger）” 是英国首艘击败美国船只的快速帆船，因此答案为 “Challenger”。

（4）题目 10：Competition increased when additional Chinese trading \___\___ were established.
题目翻译：当中国新增贸易_\___\__建立后，竞争加剧。
答案：ports（港口）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “it was really ignited when new ports were opened up for trade in China”，“new ports” 对应 “additional... ports”，明确提到中国新增贸易港口引发竞争加剧，因此答案为 “ports”。

（5）题目 11：Merchants were occasionally in such a hurry that they failed to complete the \___\___ before leaving China.
题目翻译：商人有时过于匆忙，离开中国前未能完成_\___\__。
答案：paperwork（文件手续）
解析：定位 Paragraph 7 中 “sometimes not even taking time to fill out the official paperwork”，“fill out the official paperwork” 对应 “complete the paperwork”，明确提到商人来不及完成官方文件手续，因此答案为 “paperwork”。

（6）题目 12：At the end of their journey, the ships needed the help of \___\___.
题目翻译：航程结束时，船只需要_\___\__的帮助。
答案：tugs（拖船）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “once there, they would be towed by tugs up the river and into the docks”，“towed by tugs” 对应 “needed the help of tugs”，明确提到船只需拖船协助进入码头，因此答案为 “tugs”。

（7）题目 13：The crews were motivated by both \___\___ and their enthusiasm for the competition.
题目翻译：船员的动力既来自_\___\__，也来自对竞争的热情。
答案：money（金钱）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “the cargo of the winning ship could earn a premium... and so the captain and crew were rewarded... But the races were about more than just money”，明确提到船员会获得金钱奖励，且竞争不止关乎金钱，即动力来自 “金钱” 与热情，因此答案为 “money”。
